page: Amazon Web Services c8gd.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Built on the arm64 architecture, this server features 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz, with no hyperthreading. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core.

For local storage, the instance includes 2850 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network performance is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 22 Gbps. In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates top-tier multi-core and memory performance. Specifically, it achieves top 10% results in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, Geekbench multi-core scores, and software compilation using Clang. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 381.77 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 181.15 GB/sec for large-block write operations, both ranking in the top tier.

The hardware profile delivers strong database and web serving capabilities. Passmark database operations and static web serving throughput both rank in the top 10% of tested servers, while Redis SET operations reach 99.59 million operations per second. Single-core performance, such as Geekbench single-core and stress-ng single-core metrics, falls within the average range. This resource configuration represents a cost-efficient option for multi-threaded, compute-heavy workloads requiring fast local storage access, though it presents a memory-capacity tradeoff for memory-bound applications. Appropriate workloads include database hosting, software compilation, high-throughput web serving, and multi-threaded data decompression.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.12xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance powered by a 48-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 96.0 GB of memory, 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers dedicated CPU allocation with one thread per core. Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in multi-core processing, software compilation, and memory bandwidth, alongside strong database operations and static web serving throughput. Conversely, single-core CPU metrics and text processing fall into the average range. This hardware profile offers a balanced, cost-efficient solution for multi-threaded workloads that benefit from high-speed local storage. It is highly suited for database hosting, software compilation, multi-threaded decompression, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.12xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized server with 48 Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 96 GB RAM, and 2850 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.

tagline: An arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated Graviton processors, high-speed local NVMe storage, and top-tier multi-core performance.

96.0 GB of memory paired with 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier multi-core performance, software compilation, and memory bandwidth
